title: WEBHOOK_URL not reflecting custom domain in Docker setup
symptom: The WEBHOOK_URL remains set to 'http://localhost' despite configuring environment variables for a custom domain. This prevents external access to webhook endpoints.
applies_when: Setting up a custom domain with reverse proxy
not_this_if: WEBHOOK_URL is correctly set in the docker-compose file
Using default settings without custom domain
root_cause: Docker does not automatically load environment variables defined in the .env file unless they are explicitly referenced in the docker-compose.yml file. This can lead to the application using default values instead of the intended custom configurations.
fix_steps: Edit the docker-compose.yml file to include 'WEBHOOK_URL' without a value
Ensure other relevant environment variables like 'N8N_PROTOCOL', 'DOMAIN_NAME', and 'SUBDOMAIN' are also included without values
Run 'docker compose down' and then 'docker compose up -d --build' to apply changes
agent_instruction: Update the docker-compose file to reference WEBHOOK_URL and other necessary environment variables without values, then rebuild the Docker containers.
